heading: Election of taxable year other than required taxable year
label: 444 Election of taxable year other than required taxable year
notes: Amendments1988—Subsec. (a). , substituted “as otherwise provided in this section” for “as provided in subsections (b) and (c)”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 2004(e)(1)(A)Subsec. (b)(4). , inserted “except as provided in regulations,” before “the term”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 2004(e)(13)Subsec. L. 100–647, title II, § 2004(e)(11)102 Stat. 3602Dec. 22, 1987Nov. 10, 1988“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in this subsection, the amendments made by this section [enacting this section and sections 280H and 7519 of this title] shall apply to taxable years beginning after .December 31, 1986“(2).—Required paymentsThe amendments made by subsection (b) [enacting ] shall apply to applicable election years beginning after .section 7519 of this titleDecember 31, 1986“(3).—ElectionsAny election under section 444 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by subsection (a)) for an entity’s 1st taxable year beginning after , shall not be required to be made before the 90th day after the date of the enactment of this Act [].December 31, 1986Dec. 22, 1987“(4).—Special rule for existing entities electing s corporation statusIf a C corporation (within the meaning of section 1361(a)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) with a taxable year other than the calendar year—“(A)made an election after , and before , under section 1362 of such Code to be treated as an S corporation, andSeptember 18, 1986January 1, 1988“(B)elected to have the calendar year as the taxable year of the S corporation,then section 444(b)(2)(B) of such Code shall be applied by taking into account the deferral period of the last taxable year of the C corporation rather than the deferral period of the taxable year being changed. The preceding sentence shall apply only in the case of an election under section 444 of such Code made for a taxable year beginning before 1989.”

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, a partnership, S corporation, or personal service corporation may elect to have a taxable year other than the required taxable year.

children.heading: Limitations on taxable years which may be elected
children.label: b Limitations on taxable years which may be elected

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (2) and (3), an election may be made under subsection (a) only if the deferral period of the taxable year elected is not longer than 3 months.

children.label: B
children.text: the deferral period of the taxable year which is being changed.

children.heading: Special rule for entities retaining 1986 taxable years
children.label: 3 Special rule for entities retaining 1986 taxable years
children.text: In the case of an entity’s 1st taxable year beginning after , an entity may elect a taxable year under subsection (a) which is the same as the entity’s last taxable year beginning in 1986.December 31, 1986

children.label: A
children.text: the beginning of such year, and

children.label: B
children.text: the close of the 1st required taxable year ending within such year.
in the case of a partnership or S corporation, such entity shall make the payments required by section 7519, and
in the case of a personal service corporation, such corporation shall be subject to the deduction limitations of section 280H.

children.heading: Person making election
children.label: 1 Person making election
children.text: An election under subsection (a) shall be made by the partnership, S corporation, or personal service corporation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any election under subsection (a) shall remain in effect until the partnership, S corporation, or personal service corporation changes its taxable year or otherwise terminates such election. Any change to a required taxable year may be made without the consent of the Secretary.

children.heading: No further election
children.label: B No further election
children.text: If an election is terminated under subparagraph (A) or paragraph (3)(A), the partnership, S corporation, or personal service corporation may not make another election under subsection (a).

children.label: i
children.text: no election may be under subsection (a) with respect to any entity which is part of a tiered structure, and

children.label: ii
children.text: an election under subsection (a) with respect to any entity shall be terminated if such entity becomes part of a tiered structure.

children.heading: Exceptions for structures consisting of certain entities with same taxable year
children.label: B Exceptions for structures consisting of certain entities with same taxable year
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any tiered structure which consists only of partnerships or S corporations (or both) all of which have the same taxable year.

children.heading: Required taxable year
children.label: e Required taxable year
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “required taxable year” means the taxable year determined under section 706(b), 1378, or 441(i) without taking into account any taxable year which is allowable by reason of business purposes. Solely for purposes of the preceding sentence, sections 706(b), 1378, and 441(i) shall be treated as in effect for taxable years beginning before .January 1, 1987

children.heading: Personal service corporation
children.label: f Personal service corporation
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “personal service corporation” has the meaning given to such term by section 441(i)(2).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: g Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section, including regulations to prevent the avoidance of subsection (b)(2)(B) or (d)(2)(B) through the change in form of an entity.
